label: Software / tool review
opening_rule: First sentence: whether this software solves the problem it claims to solve -- not what the software is or who made it.
closing_rule: Final paragraph: specific recommendation -- for which workflow, at which price point, compared to which alternative. One sentence that a reader can act on.

sections.name: What it does
sections.purpose: The problem it solves, the target user, the core feature set. State immediately whether the positioning is accurate or inflated.

sections.name: How it works
sections.purpose: Key workflows, UI/UX, integration with existing tools. Name specific friction points and what they cost in time or effort.

sections.name: Performance and reliability
sections.purpose: Speed, stability, edge case handling. Name specific failure modes encountered. If it crashes -- say when and how often.

sections.name: Pricing and value
sections.purpose: Cost structure -- subscription, one-time, freemium. Is the pricing honest for what is delivered? Name direct competitors and their pricing.

sections.name: Problems
sections.purpose: Missing features, bad design decisions, vendor lock-in, privacy concerns. Do not soften. If open-source alternatives exist -- name them.

sections.name: Verdict
sections.purpose: For whom is this worth paying for, and at what price point? Name the specific workflow where it wins and the specific workflow where a competitor is better. No generic praise.
